# zidl — C++ Backend Reference

Generated by `zidl -b cpp`. Produces a `.hpp` header plus a companion
`_cdr.cpp` source file containing CDR serialize/deserialize implementations.
When `--generate-interfaces` is enabled it also emits an `_impl.cpp` source
file with C ABI-backed concrete `FooImpl` classes. Uses the IDL4-native C++
mapping (formal/25-03-03, C++11 minimum).

---

## Generated Files

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `<name>.hpp` | Type declarations + CDR function declarations |
| `<name>_cdr.cpp` | CDR serialize/deserialize implementations |
| `<name>_impl.cpp` | C ABI-backed interface implementations, when `--generate-interfaces` is set |

`--split-files` produces one `.hpp`/`_cdr.cpp` pair per top-level named type.
The generated CDR functions are declarations in the header and definitions in
the companion source file.

---

## Type Mapping

| IDL type | C++ type |
|---|---|
| `boolean` | `bool` |
| `octet` | `uint8_t` |
| `char` | `char` |
| `wchar` | `wchar_t` |
| `short` / `int16` | `int16_t` |
| `unsigned short` / `uint16` | `uint16_t` |
| `long` / `int32` | `int32_t` |
| `unsigned long` / `uint32` | `uint32_t` |
| `long long` / `int64` | `int64_t` |
| `unsigned long long` / `uint64` | `uint64_t` |
| `float` | `float` |
| `double` | `double` |
| `long double` | `long double` |
| `fixed<D,S>` | `double` |
| `string` (unbounded) | `std::string` |
| `string<N>` (bounded) | `std::string` (bound enforced at CDR serialize time) |
| `wstring` | `std::wstring` |
| `sequence<T>` | `std::vector<T>` |
| `T[N]` | `std::array<T, N>` |
| `map<K,V>` | `std::map<K, V>` |
| `@optional T` | `std::optional<T>` |
| `enum` | `enum class Foo : uint32_t { ... }` |
| `bitmask` | `enum class Foo : uint8/16/32/64_t { ... }` |
| `bitset` | `struct Foo` with integer storage member |
| `struct` | `struct Foo` (with `: public Base` for inheritance) |
| `union` | `class Foo` with `_d()` accessor + anonymous union |
| IDL module | C++ namespace |

---

## Generated File Structure

```cpp
#pragma once  // or #ifndef guard

#include <cstdint>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <optional>
#include <array>
#include <map>
#include "zidl_cdr.h"

namespace MyModule {

struct MyStruct {
    int32_t x{};
    std::string name{};
    std::optional<double> opt_val{};
};

int MyStruct_serialize(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const MyStruct *v);
int MyStruct_deserialize(ZidlCdrReader *r, MyStruct *v);

// For keyed types:
int MyStruct_serialize_key(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const MyStruct *v);
int MyStruct_deserialize_key(ZidlCdrReader *r, MyStruct *v);
int MyStruct_compute_key_hash(const MyStruct *v, uint8_t hash[16]);

} // namespace MyModule
```

Use `--c-pragma-once` to emit `#pragma once` instead of the default `#ifndef` guards.
Use `--cpp-namespace <ns>` to wrap all output in an outer namespace.
Use `--c-export-macro <MACRO>` to prepend a DLL export macro to all CDR function declarations.

---

## CDR Serialize/Deserialize Signatures

Serialize and deserialize are free functions overloaded per type:

```cpp
/* Serialize v into the CDR writer w. */
int Foo_serialize(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const Foo *v);

/* Deserialize from CDR reader r into v. */
int Foo_deserialize(ZidlCdrReader *r, Foo *v);
```

---

## Key Serialization

For structs with `@key` members:

```cpp
/* Serialize key fields only as canonical PLAIN_CDR2 big-endian */
int Foo_serialize_key(ZidlCdrWriter *w, const Foo *v);

/* Deserialize key fields from a full CDR stream; non-key fields skipped */
int Foo_deserialize_key(ZidlCdrReader *r, Foo *v);

/* Compute RTPS key hash: key ≤ 16 bytes → zero-pad; > 16 bytes → MD5 */
int Foo_compute_key_hash(const Foo *v, uint8_t hash[16]);
```

---

## `@verbatim` Injection (not yet implemented)

`@verbatim` annotations are parsed and preserved in the IR as raw annotations
(accessible via `TypeAnnotations.raw`), but the C++ backend does not yet read
or emit them. The planned placement positions are `BEGIN_FILE`,
`BEFORE_DECLARATION`, `BEGIN_DECLARATION`, `END_DECLARATION`,
`AFTER_DECLARATION`, and `END_FILE`, filtered by `language == "c++"` or
`language == "*"`.

---

## `zidl-cdr` Dependency

The generated `.hpp` includes `zidl_cdr.h` and uses `ZidlCdrWriter`, `ZidlCdrReader`,
`zidl_cdr_compute_key_hash`, and `zidl_md5` from `zidl-cdr`. `std::string`,
`std::vector<T>`, and `std::map<K,V>` members use default STL allocators; custom
allocator support (STL allocator template parameters or `std::pmr`) is not yet implemented.

Build by including the header in your C++ project and compiling the generated
`<name>_cdr.cpp` source alongside `packages/zidl-cdr/src/zidl_cdr.c`. Add
`packages/zidl-cdr/include` to your include paths.

---

## Backend-Specific Options

| Option | Effect |
|---|---|
| `--c-pragma-once` | Emit `#pragma once` instead of `#ifndef` guards |
| `--c-header-guard-prefix <pfx>` | Prefix for `#ifndef` include guards |
| `--cpp-namespace <ns>` | Wrap all output in an outer namespace |
| `--c-export-macro <macro>` | DLL export macro prepended to CDR function declarations |
| `--no-typesupport` | Suppress CDR serialize/deserialize output |
| `--generate-zzdds-wrappers` | Emit typed zzdds TypeSupport/DataWriter/DataReader wrappers for keyed, non-mutable topic structs |

---

## Migration Notes

`--generate-interfaces` now emits C ABI-backed concrete classes named
`FooImpl` instead of the earlier `FooZigImpl` spelling. Downstream code that
names those generated implementation classes directly should rename those
references.

---

## Known Limitations

| Feature | Status |
|---|---|
| Custom allocators for `std::string` / `std::vector` / `std::map` | Not yet implemented — all STL containers use default allocators; `std::pmr` or allocator template parameter support is planned |
| `@verbatim` annotations | Parsed and stored in IR but not yet injected into generated output — see [`@verbatim` Injection](#verbatim-injection-not-yet-implemented) above |
| Union discriminant: complex types | Emits `/* TODO: unsupported discriminant */` |
| `--generate-interfaces`: complex-type adaptation | Primitive and string operation signatures are adapted; richer signatures emit `/* TODO: adapt C++ types */` stubs |
